entries.garrytan/gbrain: An opinionated brain for Hermes Agent featuring a self-wiring knowledge graph with automated entity extraction. It combines vector retrieval with backlink-boosted ranking and includes 26 specialized skills for media enrichment.
entries.vectorize-io/hindsight: A biomimetic memory system that implements retain, recall, and reflect workflows for long-term learning. It simplifies integration via a two-line LLM wrapper for automatic memory management.
entries.greyhaven-ai/autocontext: A recursive context harness that uses multi-agent loops to extract and persist validated knowledge across complex tasks. It can distill stable behaviors into local models through MLX integration.
entries.stephenschoettler/hermes-lcm: A lossless context management plugin utilizing a hierarchical DAG-based engine for multi-level compaction. It ensures data integrity via immutable-first SQLite storage so no message is ever deleted.
entries.elkimek/honcho-self-hosted: A private, self-hosted backend that enables cross-session persistence for Hermes Agent. It supports local inference servers or OpenAI-compatible providers and deploys via Docker, PostgreSQL, and Redis.
entries.yoloshii/ClawMem: A local-first context engine that uses a unified SQLite vault for on-device memory persistence. It employs a hybrid retrieval strategy combining vector search, BM25, and graph traversal.
entries.plur-ai/plur: A shared memory layer for multi-agent systems that stores persistent data as open-format YAML engrams. It features zero-cost hybrid retrieval and a system for memory decay and strengthening based on usage.
entries.amanning3390/flowstate-qmd: An anticipatory memory system that prefetches context into a local intuition file for faster retrieval. Built for privacy with SQLite-vec and node-llama-cpp, it integrates as an MCP server for coding workflows.

entries.mukul975/Anthropic-Cybersecurity-Skills: This repository provides 754 structured cybersecurity skills mapped to major industry frameworks like MITRE ATT&CK and NIST. It equips Hermes with specialized domain knowledge across 26 security sectors, ensuring high-fidelity performance in defensive and offensive security tasks.
entries.conorbronsdon/avoid-ai-writing: This tool audits and rewrites content to eliminate 36 distinct AI writing patterns and structural tells. By using a three-tier word replacement system and a second-pass audit, it helps Hermes produce more natural, human-like prose that bypasses linguistic detection.
entries.wondelai/skills: A modular, cross-platform library that brings standardized skills for architecture, UX design, and product strategy to Hermes. It utilizes the agentskills.io specification, allowing for easy installation of professional-grade capabilities via a dedicated CLI.
entries.DougTrajano/pydantic-ai-skills: This project implements the Agent Skills open specification for Pydantic AI with a focus on efficiency. It uses progressive disclosure of metadata to reduce token usage while supporting both programmatic and filesystem-based skill definitions.
entries.Agents365-ai/drawio-skill: This skill enables Hermes to generate complex draw.io diagrams, including UML and system architectures, from natural language descriptions. It features six specialized presets and supports grid-aligned layouts with animated connectors for professional visual documentation.
entries.smartcontractkit/chainlink-agent-skills: Designed for blockchain interaction, this skill set allows Hermes to manage Chainlink Runtime Environment and CCIP workflows. It follows the agentskills.io spec to facilitate cross-chain sends, contract discovery, and real-time monitoring.
entries.tlehman/litprog-skill: This literate programming skill allows Hermes to weave runnable code and prose together into synchronized .lit.md files. It includes a tangler to extract source files and automatically syncs edits between documentation and codebases.
entries.esaradev/icarus-plugin: Icarus provides Hermes with a self-memory system that uses markdown-based directories to share context across different instances. It features an end-to-end pipeline for extracting decision-quality data to train and evaluate replacement models.
entries.chigwell/skilldock.io: This registry acts as a discovery and management hub for reusable AI skills using an OpenAPI-driven Python SDK. It supports recursive dependency resolution and includes integrated commerce features for monetizing skills via TON and Stripe.
entries.black-forest-labs/skills: The official skill set for FLUX image generation, providing Hermes with precise prompting guidelines and API integration patterns. It supports structured JSON prompting and hex color specifications to ensure high-quality, predictable visual outputs.
entries.Romanescu11/hermes-skill-factory: This meta-skill plugin monitors active workflows to automatically detect and propose new, reusable skills. It generates both the necessary SKILL.md documentation and Python scaffolds, allowing Hermes to programmatically expand its own capabilities.
entries.ReinaMacCredy/maestro: Maestro is a harness for long-running agents that maintains shared state on disk and facilitates context transfers via structured handoffs. It provides a read-only TUI and JSON snapshots to track complex missions and cross-feature learning.
entries.tiann/execplan-skill: This skill enables Hermes to execute complex, multi-step tasks with built-in checkpoints and recovery procedures. It tracks decisions in real-time and provides autonomous rollback capabilities to ensure safe execution of long-running processes.
entries.cablate/Agentic-MCP-Skill: A progressive Model Context Protocol client that uses three-layer lazy loading to minimize token consumption for tool definitions. It maintains persistent connections to multiple MCP servers via a socket-based daemon for efficient metadata retrieval.
entries.armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge: This forge converts existing repositories and developer documentation into agentskills.io-compliant instruction sets. It maps AI instructions directly to source code line numbers, allowing Hermes to ingest and utilize specific library versions accurately.
entries.amanning3390/hermeshub: HermesHub is a community marketplace for browsing and installing skills with one-click CLI integration. It prioritizes safety by performing automated security scans for prompt injections and malicious payloads on all hosted skills.
entries.PederHP/skillsdotnet: This project brings the agentskills.io specification to the C# and .NET ecosystem, providing MCP resource templates for on-demand loading. It includes vendor-specific shortcuts to ensure Hermes remains compatible with .NET-based development environments.

entries.numtide/llm-agents.nix: This project provides automated daily updates for AI coding agent packages, including Hermes, through the Nix ecosystem. It enables instant execution via Nix flake commands, allowing developers to run the latest agent versions without manual installation or configuration.
entries.TheAiSingularity/hermesclaw: Hermesclaw focuses on high-security deployments by providing hardware-level enforcement of network, filesystem, and process boundaries. It features three security policy presets and secure gateways for integrating Hermes with communication platforms like Telegram and Discord.
entries.0xrsydn/nix-hermes-agent: This NixOS module enables declarative management of Hermes configurations, documents, and systemd services for fully reproducible deployments. It prioritizes security through sops-nix integration for secrets handling and supports a hybrid skill model for both managed and interactive installs.
entries.rookiemann/portable-hermes-agent: Designed for Windows users, this portable desktop agent requires no installation, admin rights, or Docker to provide a full GUI and over 100 integrated tools. It features a built-in workflow engine for scheduled automation and local model management with GPU monitoring.
entries.xmbshwll/hermes-agent-docker: This repository provides a streamlined Docker sandbox that bundles the official Hermes installation with mini-swe-agent. It supports persistent state through mounted directories and allows users to target specific Hermes releases using build arguments.
entries.JackTheGit/hermes-autonomous-server: Targeting production environments, this project configures the Hermes Gateway as a reboot-safe systemd service for headless Ubuntu and Debian servers. It is optimized for autonomous execution, enabling the agent to perform recurring AI cron jobs without manual intervention.
entries.ellickjohnson/portainer-stack-hermes: This deployment option uses a Docker Compose stack to provide a web-based terminal interface for Hermes via ttyd. It is designed for easy management through Portainer and utilizes GitHub Actions for automated image builds.

entries.builderz-labs/mission-control: This self-hosted orchestration platform manages multi-agent workflows using a lightweight SQLite backend with zero external dependencies. It is ideal for Hermes users who require real-time task monitoring and strict quality control through the built-in Aegis review system.
entries.swarmclawai/swarmclaw: Swarmclaw provides a self-hosted runtime for building autonomous agent swarms that support over 20 providers, including local Ollama and Hermes. It distinguishes itself by enabling agents to learn new skills from conversations and maintain persistent memory across delegated tasks.
entries.howdymary/hermes-agent-metaharness: This framework focuses on meta-optimization by using an automated outer-loop search to refine the harness code used for Hermes Agent evaluations. It orchestrates complex benchmarks like TBLite and TB2, tracking performance frontiers through structured candidate mutation.
entries.runtimenoteslabs/gladiator: Gladiator creates a competitive arena where two autonomous AI companies, powered by Hermes Agent, compete for GitHub stars. The system orchestrates nine specialized agents and visualizes their real-time execution, code generation, and scoreboard metrics on a live dashboard.
entries.1ilkhamov/opencode-hermes-multiagent: This project coordinates a massive pipeline of 17 specialized agents dedicated to tasks ranging from infrastructure to security. It enforces rigorous quality gates by routing all outputs through mandatory reviewer and tester agent loops to ensure production-ready code.
entries.supermodeltools/bigiron: Bigiron implements an AI-native software development lifecycle by providing Hermes with structural code graphs via the Supermodel MCP. It automates an eight-phase development process, using graph-diff gates to ensure architectural integrity at every step from planning to health checks.
entries.Rainhoole/hermes-agent-acp-skill: This framework utilizes ACP-style delegation to route tasks between Hermes subagents and external models like Codex or Claude Code. It prioritizes operational safety by isolating delegation workflows to prevent context pollution and enforcing strict timeouts and output limits.

entries.junhoyeo/tokscale: This Rust-based TUI tracks token consumption and real-time costs across over 20 AI coding agents, including Hermes. It provides developers with detailed usage analytics and 3D visualizations by leveraging LiteLLM pricing data to monitor expenses across multiple CLI tools.
entries.joeynyc/hermes-skins: This utility allows developers to personalize the Hermes terminal interface through YAML-based configuration files for ASCII banners, spinners, and color schemes. It simplifies theme creation by allowing custom skins to inherit default values, ensuring a consistent visual experience across the ecosystem.
entries.AlexAI-MCP/hermes-CCC: This project ports the Hermes Agent brain and its 46 core skills directly into the Claude Code Channel for native execution without external processes or OAuth. It specifically enhances the developer workflow by integrating MLOps tools for vLLM inference, GGUF management, and reinforcement learning training.
entries.unmodeled-tyler/vessel-browser: Vessel is an AI-native browser designed for autonomous agent control, exposing browser state and navigation through Model Context Protocol (MCP) endpoints. It provides a human-visible interface for auditing agent workflows and supports persistent sessions with named checkpoints and cookie storage.
entries.Abruptive/Ankh.md: This framework enables the creation of scoped Hermes Agents that are localized to specific project folders with their own memories, tools, and instructions. It allows for seamless switching between global and project-local configurations, ensuring agents remain context-aware within a defined workspace.
entries.roli-lpci/lintlang: A static analysis tool that identifies structural language bugs in agent configurations and system prompts using seven specialized detectors. It operates with zero LLM calls to ensure data privacy and provides verdict-based gating for CI/CD pipelines to prevent faulty agent logic from being deployed.
entries.0xNyk/openclaw-to-hermes: This migration utility automates the transition from OpenClaw to Hermes Agent by mapping personas, memories, and complex model configurations into the Hermes YAML format. It ensures a smooth onboarding process by handling the transfer of skills, workspace files, and local environment credentials.
entries.42-evey/evey-setup: This deployment script provides a turnkey Docker stack for Hermes Agent, integrating LiteLLM, Ollama, and Qdrant for local GPU inference. It comes pre-configured with 29 community plugins for memory and social validation, enabling a full-featured agent environment at zero cost.

entries.nesquena/hermes-webui: This zero-build web interface replicates the full Hermes Agent CLI experience through a functional three-panel layout for sessions, chat, and file browsing. It is designed for simplicity and accessibility, allowing users to manage their workspace from any browser or mobile device without complex installation steps.
entries.outsourc-e/hermes-workspace: A mobile-first PWA that provides a native-feeling web workspace featuring real-time SSE streaming for tool calls and agent outputs. It distinguishes itself with comprehensive memory and skills management tools, allowing users to search, edit, and inspect the agent's internal state directly.
entries.xaspx/hermes-control-interface: This self-hosted dashboard acts as a multi-agent gateway for starting, configuring, and monitoring several agent profiles simultaneously. It stands out by offering detailed token analytics and cost tracking alongside system metrics and cron job management.
entries.dodo-reach/hermes-desktop: A native macOS workspace that utilizes direct SSH connections to interact with Hermes without requiring local file mirroring or background daemons. It features a multi-tabbed terminal with customizable themes, providing a high-performance environment for managing sessions and skills.
entries.clawvader-tech/hermes-telegram-miniapp: This React-based Telegram Mini App brings a terminal-style chat interface and real-time system monitoring to the Telegram ecosystem. It supports background agent spawning for up to five concurrent instances, making it ideal for mobile-centric agent orchestration.
entries.fathah/hermes-desktop: Designed as a companion app, this tool simplifies the initial setup by providing guided installation and dependency resolution for Hermes Agent. It bridges the gap between different LLM providers and local endpoints while offering integrated messaging gateways for Discord and Telegram.
entries.sanchomuzax/hermes-webui: A monitoring-focused dashboard that provides a real-time overview of session activity, gateway status, and accumulated costs. It leverages SQLite to enable full-text search across conversation histories, making it a powerful tool for inspecting past agent interactions.
entries.Euraika-Labs/pan-ui: This self-hosted workspace features an integrated marketplace for discovering and installing new skills and MCP servers. It streamlines the deployment process with an automated setup wizard that supports daemon and systemd execution for persistent agent operations.
entries.pyrate-llama/hermes-ui: A lightweight, glassmorphic web interface built on a single-file React architecture that requires no build step. It features a dedicated artifact panel for live rendering of HTML, SVG, and code, providing immediate visual feedback for agent-generated content.
